content: Diabetes prevention assessment includes: (1) Screening criteria—USPSTF recommends screening adults 35-70 years with overweight/obesity; ADA recommends screening all adults ≥35 years, earlier with risk factors (family history, race/ethnicity, gestational diabetes history, PCOS, hypertension, dyslipidemia); (2) Diagnostic tests—fasting plasma glucose (100-125 mg/dL prediabetes, ≥126 diabetes), HbA1c (5.7-6.4% prediabetes, ≥6.5% diabetes), 2-hour OGTT (140-199 mg/dL prediabetes, ≥200 diabetes); (3) Risk stratification—ADA Diabetes Risk Test, FINDRISC, QDiabetes risk scores identifying candidates for intensive intervention; (4) Monitoring—annual screening for prediabetes progression, weight tracking, lifestyle behavior assessment; (5) Population surveillance—NHANES prevalence data, BRFSS behavioral indicators, national diabetes registries. Continuous glucose monitoring emerging for prediabetes research. Limitations include HbA1c variability by race/ethnicity, anemia affecting accuracy, and discordance between diagnostic tests.
